description: The capability being invoked (e.g. book_flight, deploy_service)
Side-effect posture of the capability (none, reversible, irreversible)
Estimated monetary or resource cost of the invocation
Maximum budget the delegating principal has authorized
Whether required permissions are available, restricted, or denied
Stable identifier for the logical task spanning multiple invocations
Risk classification for the operation (low, medium, high)
OAuth-style scopes carried by the delegation token
The action must not proceed under the given constraints
The task_id must be preserved across the invocation chain
Parent invocation ID must be propagated for lineage reconstruction
A durable audit record must be emitted for the invocation
The failure response must explain why the budget was exceeded
The failure response must explain which authority or permission is missing
On failure, escalate to a principal or replan rather than retrying blindly
ANIP exposes cost metadata before invocation so agents can reason about budget
ANIP exposes side-effect posture so agents can assess risk before acting
ANIP returns machine-readable failure details with recovery guidance
ANIP supports preflight permission checks before invocation
ANIP propagates task_id across invocations for correlation
ANIP propagates parent_invocation_id for lineage reconstruction
Audit records are searchable and queryable after the fact
ANIP provides actionable guidance on how to resolve failures
ANIP provides machine-readable pointers for cross-service verification
